items.titleEn: Bieber finding vintage form again with season-best 7-inning gem
bodyEn: TORONTO -- Despite getting off to a slow start when he initially returned from the injured list in June, Shane Bieber looks to have found his groove for the Blue Jays.
That continued on Thursday, as Bieber silenced the American League-leading Rays bats over seven innings of one-run ball at Rogers Centre while surrendering just three hits in Toronto’s 3-1 victory.
The win snapped the Blue Jays’ four-game losing streak and ensured Toronto wasn’t swept by its division rival at home.

Bieber needed just 86 pitches (57 strikes) to get through his season-high in innings pitched, striking out three during an outing where he relied heavily upon his fastball. The 31-year-old threw his heater 33 times -- averaging 92.5 mph with the offering -- while mixing in a variety of changeups, cutters, knuckle curves, sinkers and sliders.

The only run Tampa Bay managed came after a two-out rally in the third inning, when Hunter Feduccia doubled off the right-field wall before Yandy Díaz brought him home with a single on a 1-0 breaking ball.

After allowing 12 combined runs through his first three starts off the injured list, Bieber has now rattled off three consecutive positive outings as he continues to take steps in the right direction now that he’s settled back into the Major Leagues.

Bieber seemingly began his turnaround in a July 10 start against the Padres, where he settled in after two first-inning runs before shutting down San Diego for the next 3 2/3 frames. He then came out of the break with six scoreless frames against the White Sox on Saturday, striking out a season-best six batters.

“I think that he's kind of been trending up from his outing in San Diego to his last one,” Blue Jays manager John Schneider said of Bieber pregame. “I think that he's kind of kicked the rust off a little bit from the layoff, and he's in a better spot delivery-wise.”

Toronto delivered enough offense to back its starter on Thursday, thanks to some heads-up baserunning from Ernie Clement in the first inning and RBI singles from George Springer and Vladimir Guerrero Jr. in the third and seventh innings.

Clement opened the scoring when he raced around from second after an Alejandro Kirk infield hit got away from Rays third baseman Junior Caminero, allowing the Blue Jays to steal a run right out of the gate.
items.titleZh: 比伯重现巅峰，赛季最佳7局精彩表现
items.summaryZh: 比伯7局失1分，助蓝鸟3-1胜光芒，终结4连败。
bodyZh: 多伦多——尽管6月从伤病名单回归后开局缓慢，但Shane Bieber似乎已为蓝鸟找到了节奏。
周四，比伯在罗杰斯中心主投7局仅失1分，只被敲出3支安打，压制了美国联盟领先的光芒打线，帮助多伦多3-1获胜。
这场胜利终结了蓝鸟的四连败，并确保多伦多未在主场被分区对手横扫。
比伯仅用86球（57好球）就完成了赛季最高的投球局数，他大量依赖速球，三振了3名打者。这位31岁投手投了33颗速球，平均球速92.5英里/小时，同时混合了各种变速球、卡特球、指叉曲球、伸卡球和滑球。
坦帕湾的唯一得分来自第三局两出局后的反攻，Hunter Feduccia击出右外野墙上的二垒安打，随后Yandy Díaz在1-0的变速球上击出一垒安打将他送回本垒。
在从伤病名单回归后的前三场先发中共失12分后，比伯现已连续三场表现出色，随着他重新适应大联盟，正在朝正确方向不断迈进。
比伯似乎从7月10日对教士的先发开始反弹，当时他在首局失2分后稳住阵脚，随后3又2/3局封锁了圣迭戈。全明星赛后，他在周六对白袜的比赛中投了6局无失分，三振了赛季最高的6名打者。
“我认为他从圣地亚哥那场到最近一场一直在上升，”蓝鸟总教练John Schneider赛前谈到比伯时说。“我认为他因休战而生疏的状态已有所恢复，投球机制也更好了。”
多伦多周四也为先发投手提供了足够的火力支持，这得益于Ernie Clement在第一局机智的跑垒，以及George Springer和Vladimir Guerrero Jr.分别在第三局和第七局击出的打点一垒安打。
Clement率先得分，他乘Alejandro Kirk的内野安打被光芒三垒手Junior Caminero漏接之机，从二垒跑回本垒，让蓝鸟一开场就偷到一分。

items.titleEn: Blue Jays announce signing of top pick Carlon, 16 others from Draft class
bodyEn: TORONTO -- The Blue Jays have agreed to a $2.4 million bonus with their first pick from the 2026 MLB Draft, lefty Cole Carlon.

The bonus, first reported by MLB Pipeline’s Jim Callis, comes in just below the slot value for the No. 39 pick, which is $2,571,700. The Blue Jays did not pick in the first round because they, along with the Dodgers, Mets, Phillies and Yankees, received a 10-pick penalty on their first pick for exceeding the second surcharge threshold of the Competitive Balance Tax, which bumped them back to No. 39.

Carlon is a powerhouse on the mound, every bit of 6-foot-5, 230 pounds with the ability to touch triple digits. Callis also calls his slider the best in the MLB Draft, and the Blue Jays were thrilled to land the Arizona State product where they did, expecting him to be off the board by the time they got their first crack at the Draft.

“One of my friends texted me after, and I was laughing about the way he put it. He said that ‘He’s got two October pitches,’” said director of amateur scouting Marc Tramuta. “I felt like that was a good characterization of the fastball and slider that are currently in place. We just like the big physicality at 6-foot-5. We think there’s some athleticism to his delivery.”

Carlon’s 133 strikeouts over just 83 2/3 innings ranked fifth in Division 1 this past NCAA season, and given the Blue Jays’ success with Trey Yesavage coming out of college, it’s easy to dream on how quickly Carlon could climb the ladder.

Given his NCAA workload this season, which was already ramping up from a bullpen role in previous seasons, don’t expect the Blue Jays to push Carlon much more in 2026. It’s possible he’s shut down entirely and spends the rest of this summer focused on development at the club’s complex in Dunedin, Fla., but once Carlon gets on a mound, he’ll be a must-watch prospect in this system.

“He throws backwards. He throws his slider more than any other pitch,” said Baseball America’s Carlos Collazo on MLB Network’s Draft coverage. “He throws that about 52% of the time. You look at how big this guy is, I see Robbie Ray, just a bigger version of him. The Blue Jays had [Ray]. This guy is going to come at you, but you’re going to be guessing. Are you going to get a 100 mph pitch or are you going to get a slider that has incredible movement?”

Money matters, of course, especially with the Blue Jays having the second-smallest bonus pool in the Draft. Coming in roughly $170,000 below the slot value on Carlon’s pick is valuable, along with some of the bonus pool money the Blue Jays are expected to save in rounds four through 10, where they went heavy on college picks. All of these arrows point to catcher Will Brick, the high schooler who represents the Blue Jays’ “big swing” of this class.

Brick was widely considered the best prep catcher in this class and had been connected to the Blue Jays as early as the No. 39 pick, but they landed him in the fourth round (No. 131) and are expected to make an aggressive, over-slot play to land him. An excellent defensive catcher, Brick’s power also has Tramuta and the Blue Jays’ scouting group excited. Presuming the Blue Jays pull this off, which would be a major win for their farm system, Brick would immediately become one of the highest-upside position players in Toronto’s Top 30.
items.titleZh: 蓝鸟宣布签约首轮新秀Carlon及其他16名选秀球员
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟以240万美元签约首轮投手Cole Carlon，并有望签下高中捕手Will Brick。
bodyZh: 多伦多 -- 蓝鸟已与2026年MLB选秀的首轮新秀、左投手Cole Carlon达成240万美元签约金的协议。
该签约金由MLB Pipeline的Jim Callis最先报道，略低于第39顺位的对应奖金（2,571,700美元）。蓝鸟未能在首轮选秀，因为他们与道奇、大都会、费城人和洋基一样，因超过竞争平衡税的第二门槛而受到首轮顺位倒退10位的处罚，从而掉到第39顺位。
Carlon是一名强大的投手，身高6英尺5英寸（约1.96米），体重230磅（约104公斤），能投出三位数的速球。Callis还称他的滑球是本MLB选秀中最好的，蓝鸟非常高兴能在该顺位选中这位亚利桑那州立大学的球员，原本预计他会在蓝鸟首次选秀前就被挑走。
“选秀后我一个朋友给我发短信，我觉得他的说法很有趣。他说‘他有两个十月级别的球路’，”蓝鸟业余球探总监Marc Tramuta说。“我认为这很好地描述了他现有的速球和滑球。我们喜欢他6英尺5英寸的大体格，我们认为他的投球动作具有运动能力。”
Carlon在2026年NCAA赛季中，仅用83 2/3局就投出133次三振，在全美一级联赛中排名第五。考虑到蓝鸟此前成功培养大学出身的Trey Yesavage，不难想象Carlon能多快晋升。
鉴于他本赛季在NCAA的投球量已经比之前几个赛季作为牛棚投手时有所增加，预计蓝鸟不会在2026年对他施加更多压力。他可能会完全休息，整个夏天在佛罗里达州达尼丁的球队基地专注于发展。但一旦Carlon站上投手丘，他将是蓝鸟系统中备受关注的潜力新秀。
“他反向配球。他投滑球的次数比其他任何球种都多，”《棒球美国》的Carlos Collazo在MLB Network的选秀报道中说。“他大约52%的时间投滑球。看看这家伙的身材，我觉得他像Robbie Ray，只是更大一号。蓝鸟曾经有过Ray。他会向你进攻，但你会猜不到：你会看到100英里的速球，还是拥有惊人位移的滑球？”
当然，金钱因素也很重要，尤其是蓝鸟的签约奖金池在选秀中位列第二小。在Carlon的顺位上省下约17万美元很有价值，此外蓝鸟预计在第4至第10轮（主要选择大学球员）也能节省部分奖金池资金。所有这些迹象都指向高中捕手Will Brick，他是蓝鸟本届选秀的‘大手笔’。
Brick被广泛认为是本届选秀中最好的高中捕手，早在第39顺位时蓝鸟就与他联系在一起，但他们最终在第4轮（第131顺位）选中他，并预计会以高于顺位价值的签约金签下他。作为一名出色的防守型捕手，Brick的力量也让Tramuta和蓝鸟球探团队感到兴奋。如果蓝鸟能成功签下他，这将是农场的重大胜利，Brick将立即成为蓝鸟top30新秀中潜力最高的位置球员之一。

items.titleEn: Amid flurry of moves, Schneider back with Blue Jays, Corbin heading to IL
bodyEn: Before Thursday's game against the Rays, the Blue Jays recalled both INF/OF Davis Schneider and LHP Adam Macko from Triple-A Buffalo, optioned INF Sean Keys to Buffalo and placed LHP Patrick Corbin on the 15-day IL with a left teres major strain, retroactive to Wednesday.

Schneider, who hit 11 homers for last year's AL champions, has hit just .153 with three home runs and 12 RBIs in 49 games with Toronto this season. He performed much better for Buffalo, hitting .253 with a .986 OPS and five homers in 30 games.
Macko is 2-0 with a 5.03 ERA in 19 games for Toronto this season. He had a 4.18 ERA in 18 appearances for Buffalo.
Corbin, in his first season with the Blue Jays, is 3-4 with a 5.51 ERA in 19 games.
Keys has hit .167 with one homer and five RBIs in 12 games with Toronto.
items.titleZh: 蓝鸟调整阵容：Schneider回归，Corbin进伤病名单
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟召回Schneider和Macko，下放Keys，Corbin因伤进15日伤病名单。
bodyZh: 在周四对阵光芒的比赛前，蓝鸟从三A水牛城召回了内/外野手Davis Schneider和左投Adam Macko，将内野手Sean Keys下放到水牛城，并将左投Patrick Corbin放入15日伤病名单，原因是左侧大圆肌拉伤，追溯至周三。
Schneider去年为美联盟冠军轰出11支本垒打，本赛季在多伦多49场比赛中仅击出.153打击率、3支本垒打和12分打点。他在水牛城表现更好，30场比赛打击率.253，OPS .986，有5支本垒打。
Macko本赛季为多伦多出战19场，战绩2胜0败，自责分率5.03。他在水牛城18次出场自责分率为4.18。
Corbin在蓝鸟的第一个赛季，19场比赛战绩3胜4败，自责分率5.51。
Keys在多伦多12场比赛中击出.167打击率，1支本垒打和5分打点。

items.titleEn: Each team's strategy heading into the Trade Deadline
bodyEn: The Aug. 3 Trade Deadline is fast approaching, and with it, potential moves that could shake up the balance of power and playoff races as we enter the regular season stretch run.
With that in mind, here's a look at each club's likely strategy for the Deadline, which could prove to be pivotal for teams that might just need an extra piece or two to make a deep October run.
Jump to: AL East | AL Central | AL West | NL East | NL Central | NL West
American League East

Blue Jays: This is beginning to feel more like a hybrid approach for the Blue Jays, who are likely sellers but could still be involved in buying some longer-term pieces. Treating this like their first crack at the offseason would be wise, with the starting rotation and outfield both clear needs in 2027 and beyond. This organization has the farm system to make (almost) any deal and can take on money, leaving it well positioned for trades with a long-term vision that don’t fall into the rental market’s traditional definition of buying and selling. There could still be room for some old-fashioned sales, though, including Kevin Gausman, Shane Bieber, Daulton Varsho, George Springer and others on expiring deals. Jeff Hoffman could be an interesting name to monitor on the relief market, too. -- Keegan Matheson

Orioles: Although the O’s strategy could still swing either way, they’re likely to buy, believing they can make a postseason push during the final two months of the season. Their recent seven-game winning streak (which was snapped Monday night in Boston) may have been the type of stretch the club needed in order to convince the front office to add to the roster by Aug. 3. There’s a chance that Baltimore will do a little bit of buying and selling, possibly making players on expiring contracts (such as outfielder Taylor Ward or left-hander Trevor Rogers) available. But it feels like the Orioles want to improve their roster in a way that makes a run into October a stronger possibility. -- Jake Rill

Rays: Buy, with an eye on a first-round bye in October. President of baseball operations Erik Neander and his staff are expected to be aggressive in addressing Tampa Bay’s multiple needs as the club has surprisingly put itself in position to contend for the AL East title. The Rays have the kind of top-end talent it takes to succeed in the postseason, but there are holes on the roster -- and they have the organizational depth to think big. They could clearly use another proven hitter behind their big three. There’s room for another bullpen arm. And another front-line starter would make them even more dangerous down the stretch. -- Adam Berry

Red Sox: The Red Sox look to be headed for the buyer’s lane in light of their recent surge, which has been one of the best in team history. Chief Baseball officer Craig Breslow is in the market for a bat. The question is: What position will that bat play? Catcher, middle infield and DH seem to be the best fits lineup-wise. Knowing full well that most of the recently successful postseason entries have one guy after another who throw heat out of the bullpen, expect Breslow to also look for a premium reliever to go with his already dominant duo of Aroldis Chapman and Garrett Whitlock. The Sox do have a strong inventory of Minor League pitching which they could deal from. -- Ian Browne

Yankees: The Yankees were already in the market for an upgrade at catcher before their lineup went cold, and now they also may be seeking another bat with Aaron Judge’s return still indefinite. A right-handed-hitting catcher to platoon with Austin Wells would be ideal. They would love to add at least one swing-and-miss reliever to their bullpen; Mason Miller is the dream scenario, but Brian Cashman has a good track record of finding in-season relief help. -- Bryan Hoch
AL Central

Guardians: The Guardians are positioned to be buyers. To what level remains to be seen. Cleveland consistently balances the present with the future, but it has a healthy farm system to make a splash for a push in a wide-open AL postseason picture. While the returns of José Ramírez and Angel Martínez will qualify as reinforcements, the Guardians could use another right-handed bat to bolster their offense and balance a lefty-heavy mix -- and any contender could benefit from added bullpen depth. An acquisition with control beyond this season could make a lot of sense. -- Tim Stebbins

Royals: Sell, but only so much. The Royals will not be in full selloff mode because they still believe in their core for 2027 and beyond. They’ll focus their efforts on trading players on expiring contracts and relievers, while trying to acquire upper-level pitching and hitting depth. The Royals will be open-minded when it comes to their veteran starters under control, Seth Lugo and Michael Wacha, but it would take a lot to move on from either one of those two. -- Anne Rogers

Tigers: Expect president of baseball operations Scott Harris and general manager Jeff Greenberg to take this decision to Deadline day, much like Dave Dombrowski did with the Tigers in 2015. Detroit's current playoff chances would point toward selling, as would the bevy of pitchers nearing free agency that the Tigers could deal for prospects, but the club's improved play over the last seven weeks gives it a better shot at a late-season run in a middling AL Wild Card race and a division in which nobody has run off. The Tigers could even strike a middle ground by trading pending free agent Tarik Skubal for a young Major League pitcher with controllable years who can help them now. -- Jason Beck

Twins: It’s trending closer to a sell, but another drastic selloff appears highly unlikely. There’s no appetite for a full rebuild. And if they get rolling again, the Twins absolutely could move back toward being buyers. The intriguing path to watch is in-between, where perhaps they trade from the Major League roster in order to add to their pitching. Walker Jenkins is waiting for an opportunity as soon as one opens on the big league roster. -- Matthew Leach

White Sox: The White Sox weren’t supposed to be this good in 2026, but here’s the latest update: They really are that good. This group has earned the right to have general manager Chris Getz supplement a playoff team. Then again, Getz and his staff have meticulously rebuilt the White Sox into a contender ahead of schedule and won’t tear that situation apart to jump up for one season. He’ll look for guys who can help now and in the future, with a possible focus on catching after Kyle Teel suffered a high left ankle sprain during Monday’s victory over the Rangers and will be sidelined for 6-12 weeks. Pitching is always a focus for any contender, but don’t look for Getz to ship away any of his young core players. -- Scott Merkin
AL West

Angels: The Angels will be sellers but it remains to be seen how aggressive they will be under interim general manager John Mozeliak. He met with the media on Monday and discussed his strategy as the club weighs if it makes sense to trade players who are under control beyond this season. Starting pitchers José Soriano and Reid Detmers are both expected to attract plenty of interest but remain under team control for two more seasons. Shortstop Zach Neto is their most valuable piece but he’s not a free agent until 2030, and making those kinds of trades at the Deadline are rare. Otherwise, they’ll look to move impending free agents like Jorge Soler, Kirby Yates and Brent Suter. -- Rhett Bollinger

Astros: The Astros will be buyers. For now. The club is still in the thick of the AL West race and will try to make some moves to upgrade if it feels it has a shot to win the division at the end of July. Houston needs a left-handed bat, relief pitching and starting pitching, though getting all three seems very unlikely. If the Astros slip to six or seven games out of first place, or more, by the Deadline, they’re likely to stand pat and not make any trades. -- Brian McTaggart

Athletics: Continuing to freefall over the past month, the A’s will likely sell, though don’t expect them to deal away any pieces of their young core. Outfielder Carlos Cortes and second baseman Jeff McNeil are players who could help a contending club. Shea Langeliers has been circulating in rumors, though it’s unlikely the A’s trade the All-Star catcher unless they get an offer that blows them away. Any moves will be geared toward acquiring pitching. -- Martín Gallegos

Mariners: They'll buy. And they could really use another power reliever -- preferably a proven one, too -- to pair with Eduard Bazardo in the setup role for closer Andrés Muñoz. They could also benefit from adding a right-handed bat, but that seems less likely, especially with a roster already crowded with position players. -- Daniel Kramer

Rangers: When president of baseball operations Chris Young addressed the media on Monday, he didn’t sound committed either way just yet. It’s understandable. The Rangers are within half a game of the lead in the AL West and remain in the thick of things when looking at the Wild Card race. But that says more about the American League than it does about the Rangers themselves, who have been hovering around .500 for much of the year. Corey Seager’s absence haunting the club’s potential is another complicating factor heading into the Deadline. -- Kennedi Landry
National League East

Braves: President of baseball operations Alex Anthopoulos is seeking a playoff-caliber starter to add to a rotation that currently has just one pitcher who would fit that description. Like every other contender, the Braves will also be in the market for a reliever. Ronald Acuña Jr.’s return from the injured list should bolster the offense. But look for Anthopoulos to also try to add a hitter who could fill the DH spot on those days when Drake Baldwin is behind the plate. -- Mark Bowman

Marlins: As part of the cluster of teams bidding for the final two NL Wild Card spots, the Marlins won’t be selling anyone, so put those Sandy Alcantara rumors away for a few more months. But president of baseball operations Peter Bendix seems more likely to make additions on the margins rather than mortgaging any of the club’s future for a rental or two. Bendix has taken a buy-and-sell approach in each of the past two years, but with the Marlins in contention this season, he could look to do more of the former than the latter. Adding a right-handed bat would seem to be a priority. -- Mark Feinsand

Mets: It’s been apparent for some time that the Mets would be sellers, though the biggest question is which players they’ll be able to move. Starters Freddy Peralta and Clay Holmes are likely to be dealt, as are relievers A.J. Minter and Brooks Raley. The intriguing question is whether any players under contract past 2027 could be in play. Would the Mets really trade Francisco Lindor? He has a full no-trade, so he controls his own fate. What about Francisco Alvarez? Right now, nearly anything is possible. -- Mark Feinsand

Nationals: The Nationals are in the Wild Card race, and their strategy depends on the team's performance leading up to Aug. 3. Unlike the past five seasons in which the Nats were definitive sellers, they could buy this year if they believe they can make a playoff push. If they decide to sell, shortstop CJ Abrams, first baseman Luis Garcia Jr. and left-hander Foster Griffin (expiring contract) are top trade candidates. -- Jessica Camerato

Phillies: The Phillies have been one of baseball’s best teams since Don Mattingly became interim manager on April 28, but they have several needs: bullpen, right-handed bat and No. 5 starter. But they also have one of baseball’s weaker farm systems, so it will be fascinating to see how much they do in regards to both quality and quantity. -- Todd Zolecki
NL Central

Brewers: They’ve been buyers almost every year since 2018, and they’ll be buyers again this year, but rarely do the Brewers make the big splash. More often, they add wisely or in the interest of depth, along the lines of Jordan Lyles when they needed pitching in 2018 or Carlos Santana and Mark Canha in 2023, when they needed bats. Those moves don’t always work -- the Brewers’ bullpen additions at the Trade Deadline have been particularly fraught -- but they also don’t come back to bite in terms of prospect losses. -- Adam McCalvy

Cardinals: St. Louis has been another surprise team in this year’s race, but president of baseball operations Chaim Bloom is unlikely to become a big buyer regardless of how close the Cardinals are to a playoff spot. Bloom could look to thread the buy-and-sell needle, with impending free agent Dustin May and Lars Nootbar serving as the most logical trade chips. Adding starting pitching depth -- preferably controllable arms -- should be the focus. -- Mark Feinsand

Cubs: The Cubs will be buyers and this stretch before the Deadline could be important for the front office in determining how hard they’ll push in the pursuit of pitching. President of baseball operations Jed Hoyer has pointed to the first-round bye odds as an indicator of aggressiveness. What is clear is Chicago needs arms -- rotation and bullpen -- given the long list of injuries that have impacted the club all season long. The Cubs will look for smaller moves in the name of depth, but expect the North Siders to keep a big move in mind. That could be an impact starter or late-inning reliever, or perhaps even both. -- Jordan Bastian

Pirates: With the Pirates in the thick of the NL Wild Card race, they’ll likely be aggressive in finding ways to improve in their clear area of need: the bullpen. Pittsburgh’s offense is among the best in baseball, and the Bucs' starting rotation is improving with Paul Skenes, Braxton Ashcraft and Jared Jones leading the way. Payroll is always a question mark for the Pirates, and they could attempt to clear some space by moving Marcell Ozuna or other bats. -- Aiden Stepansky

Reds: Based on their record, the Reds will be sellers. The only question is, how deep will they go? They have seven players who will be free agents at season's end with none of them expected to get qualifying offers if they sign elsewhere this winter. Leading this group are starting pitcher Brady Singer, slugger Eugenio Suárez and catcher Tyler Stephenson. But among players under contract or club control beyond 2026, president of baseball operations Nick Krall could get calls about super utility player Spencer Steer and starting pitcher Hunter Greene. -- Mark Sheldon
NL West

D-backs: Barring a collapse over the next 10 days, the D-backs figure to be buyers at the Trade Deadline. They have a number of pitchers returning over the next few weeks like starter Michael Soroka, closer Justin Martinez and possibly Zac Gallen. A few others, like starters Corbin Burnes and Ryne Nelson, should be ready in October. Given that as well as the performance of the bullpen to this point, the D-backs are likely to focus on adding a bat, specifically one at first base, a position that has been a sinkhole for them this year. -- Steve Gilbert

Dodgers: Even with Shohei Ohtani's pitching on hold for now and Will Smith being sidelined longer than expected, the Dodgers are more likely to improve on the margins than make any significant moves -- although they can never be counted out if the right opportunity presents itself. L.A. has the prospect capital to get big deals done, but could instead use its trade chips to make lateral moves to further strengthen the farm system. -- Sonja Chen

Giants: One of the few surefire sellers, the Giants will continue to hope they can unload one of their massive contracts (Rafael Devers, Willy Adames, Matt Chapman, Jung Hoo Lee), though it’s going to be difficult to find a match for those players. That said, San Francisco has two appealing trade chips in second baseman Luis Arraez and left-handed starter Robbie Ray, both of whom will be free agents after the season. Expect to see both rental players traded for legitimate returns. -- Mark Feinsand

Padres: The Padres want to buy. But it’s not so clear. They’re on the outside of the Wild Card race looking in, with an offense that has struggled for most of the season and a rotation with major question marks. They can address those issues at the Deadline. But only if they can assert themselves as viable contenders in the first place. The next 10 days will be critical. If they fall further back, there’s a case to sell and bring back some value for the future. There’s also a chance they stand pat. Or, they could mix and match, upgrading in some areas while dealing from others. Asked about his path earlier this month, general manager A.J. Preller noted: “All those things are on the table.” -- AJ Cassavell

Rockies: Monday’s trade, which brought Triple-A pitcher Nick Frasso and Single-A outfielder Landyn Vidourek from the Dodgers for reliever Seth Halvorsen, revealed part of the strategy. Frasso is expected in the Majors soon, Vidourek will be developed -- and they gave up the type of young, hard thrower that they clung to in the past. There are also veterans all over the roster, so starting pitchers Tomoyuki Sugano and Michael Lorenzen, relievers Brennan Bernardino and Jimmy Herget, and outfielders Jake McCarthy and Mickey Moniak could be attractive to other clubs. While the Rockies are expected to be active, it would take a massive haul for them to depart with two-time All-Star catcher Hunter Goodman. -- Thomas Harding
items.titleZh: 各队交易截止日策略
bodyZh: 8月3日的交易截止日即将到来，随着常规赛进入冲刺阶段，潜在的交易可能会改变实力平衡和季后赛格局。
有鉴于此，以下是各队在截止日可能采取的策略，这对于那些可能只需一两名额外球员就能在十月走得更远的球队来说至关重要。
跳转：美联东区 | 美联中区 | 美联西区 | 国联东区 | 国联中区 | 国联西区
美国联盟东区

蓝鸟：这越来越像一种混合策略，蓝鸟很可能是卖家，但仍可能参与购买一些长期资产。将其视为 offseason 的首次尝试是明智的，先发轮值和外野都是2027年及以后的明确需求。这支球队的农场系统足以完成（几乎）任何交易，并且有能力接手薪资，使其在长期视野的交易中处于有利位置，而不落入租借市场的传统买卖定义。不过，仍有一些传统销售的空间，包括 Kevin Gausman、Shane Bieber、Daulton Varsho、George Springer 以及其他合同即将到期的球员。Jeff Hoffman 可能是牛棚市场中值得关注的名字。-- Keegan Matheson
金莺：尽管金莺的策略仍可能摇摆，但他们很可能会买进，相信能在赛季最后两个月发起季后赛冲刺。他们最近的七连胜（周一在波士顿被终结）可能是球队所需的那种表现，以说服管理层在8月3日前补强阵容。巴尔的摩有可能进行一些买卖，可能让合同即将到期的球员（如外野手 Taylor Ward 或左投手 Trevor Rogers）进入市场。但感觉金莺希望改善阵容，使进入十月的可能性更大。-- Jake Rill

光芒：买进，着眼于十月首轮轮空。棒球运营总裁 Erik Neander 和他的团队预计将积极应对坦帕湾的多重需求，因为球队出人意料地处于争夺美联东区冠军的位置。光芒拥有在季后赛中取得成功所需的顶尖天赋，但阵容存在漏洞——而且他们有组织深度来考虑大动作。他们显然可以在三巨头之后再用一名经过验证的打者。牛棚还有空间再增加一名投手。另一名先发轮值前端的投手将让他们在冲刺阶段更具威胁。-- Adam Berry

红袜：鉴于最近的激增——这是队史最出色的表现之一——红袜似乎将进入买方行列。首席棒球官 Craig Breslow 正在寻找一名打者。问题是：这个打者打什么位置？捕手、内野中路和指定打击似乎是 lineup 中最合适的。充分了解最近成功的季后赛队伍都有一个接一个的强力牛棚投手，预计 Breslow 也会寻找一名顶级后援投手，与他已经主导的 Aroldis Chapman 和 Garrett Whitlock 组合搭档。红袜拥有大量小联盟投手资源可供交易。-- Ian Browne
洋基：在打线降温之前，洋基已经在寻求升级捕手位置，现在 Aaron Judge 的回归时间仍不确定，他们可能还需要另一个打者。一名右打捕手与 Austin Wells 轮换将是理想选择。他们希望至少为牛棚增加一名能制造挥空的后援投手；Mason Miller 是理想人选，但 Brian Cashman 在寻找赛季中期的后援投手方面有良好记录。-- Bryan Hoch
美联中区

守护者：守护者准备成为买家。但程度如何还有待观察。克利夫兰始终在现在和未来之间取得平衡，但他们拥有健康的农场系统，可以在开放的美联季后赛格局中引起轰动。虽然 José Ramírez 和 Angel Martínez 的回归将是补强，但守护者可以再使用一名右打来增强进攻并平衡左打偏多的组合——任何竞争者都可以从增加的牛棚深度中受益。拥有本赛季之后控制权的收购可能很有意义。-- Tim Stebbins
皇家：卖出，但仅限一定范围。皇家不会全面抛售，因为他们仍然相信2027年及以后的核心阵容。他们将专注于交易合同即将到期的球员和后援投手，同时尝试获得上层的小联盟投手和打者深度。皇家将对他们的受控老将先发投手 Seth Lugo 和 Michael Wacha 持开放态度，但要交易其中一个需要很大代价。-- Anne Rogers

老虎：预计棒球运营总裁 Scott Harris 和总经理 Jeff Greenberg 会将此决定推迟到截止日，就像 Dave Dombrowski 在2015年对老虎所做的那样。底特律目前的季后赛机会指向卖出，同样地，大量即将成为自由球员的投手老虎可以交易来换取潜力新秀，但球队过去七周表现提升，使其在竞争不激烈的美联外卡赛和无人脱离的赛区中有更好的机会进行赛季末冲刺。老虎甚至可以通过交易即将成为自由球员的 Tarik Skubal 来换取一名有控制年限、能立即帮助球队的年轻大联盟投手，从而达成中间立场。-- Jason Beck
双城：趋势更接近卖出，但再次大规模抛售似乎极不可能。没有全面重建的意愿。如果他们重新开始滚动，双城绝对可以回到买家行列。值得关注的有趣路径是中间路线，也许他们从大联盟阵容中交易来增加投手深度。Walker Jenkins 正在等待大联盟阵容中出现机会。-- Matthew Leach

白袜：白袜本不该在2026年这么好，但最新情况是：他们确实这么好。这支球队赢得了让总经理 Chris Getz 补充季后赛阵容的权利。不过，Getz 和他的团队将白袜精心重建为竞争者，比计划提前，他们不会为了一个赛季而拆散这个局面。他会寻找能立即和未来都有帮助的球员，可能重点关注捕手位置，因为 Kyle Teel 在周一战胜游骑兵的比赛中左脚踝高位扭伤，将缺阵6-12周。投手始终是任何竞争者关注的焦点，但不要指望 Getz 会送走任何年轻核心球员。-- Scott Merkin
美联西区

天使：天使将是卖家，但在临时总经理 John Mozeliak 领导下，他们的积极程度还有待观察。他周一与媒体会面，讨论了球队在权衡是否交易本赛季之后仍有控制权的球员时的策略。先发投手 José Soriano 和 Reid Detmers 预计都会吸引很多兴趣，但都还有两个赛季的球队控制权。游击手 Zach Neto 是他们最有价值的资产，但他在2030年之前不会成为自由球员，在截止日进行这类交易很少见。否则，他们将寻求交易即将成为自由球员的 Jorge Soler、Kirby Yates 和 Brent Suter。-- Rhett Bollinger
太空人：太空人将成为买家。目前是这样。球队仍在美联西区竞争激烈中，如果认为在七月底有机会赢得分区冠军，他们将尝试做一些升级。休斯顿需要一名左打、后援投手和先发投手，但三者都得到似乎非常不可能。如果太空人在截止日前落后分区第一6-7场或更多，他们很可能会按兵不动，不进行任何交易。-- Brian McTaggart
运动家：在过去一个月中持续自由落体，运动家很可能会卖出，但不要指望他们会交易任何年轻核心球员。外野手 Carlos Cortes 和二垒手 Jeff McNeil 是可能帮助竞争球队的球员。Shea Langeliers 一直出现在流言中，但运动家不太可能交易这位全明星捕手，除非得到让他们无法拒绝的报价。任何动作都将以获取投手为目标。-- Martín Gallegos
水手：他们将买进。他们真的可以再使用一名强力后援投手——最好是有经验的——与 Eduard Bazardo 搭档为终结者 Andrés Muñoz 布局。他们也可以从增加一名右打中受益，但这似乎不太可能，尤其阵容中位置球员已经拥挤。-- Daniel Kramer
游骑兵：当棒球运营总裁 Chris Young 周一面对媒体时，他听起来尚未承诺任何方向。这可以理解。游骑兵距离美联西区领先仅半场，在外卡争夺中也处于激烈竞争。但这更多说明美国联盟的情况，而不是游骑兵本身，他们今年大部分时间都在五成胜率附近徘徊。Corey Seager 缺席影响球队潜力，是进入截止日的另一个复杂因素。-- Kennedi Landry
国家联盟东区
勇士：棒球运营总裁 Alex Anthopoulos 正在寻找一名季后赛级别的先发投手，以加入目前只有一名符合这描述的投手的轮值。像每个竞争者一样，勇士也将寻找一名后援投手。Ronald Acuña Jr. 从伤病名单回归应该会增强进攻。但也要关注 Anthopoulos 试图增加一名打者，在 Drake Baldwin 担任捕手时担任指定打击。-- Mark Bowman
马林鱼：作为争夺最后两个国联外卡名额的球队集群的一部分，马林鱼不会出售任何人，所以把那些 Sandy Alcantara 的流言收起来几个月吧。但棒球运营总裁 Peter Bendix 更可能进行边缘补强，而不是为了租借球员而抵押球队未来。Bendix 在过去两年都采取了买卖兼顾的方式，但本赛季马林鱼在竞争中，他可能更倾向于买进。增加一名右打似乎是优先事项。-- Mark Feinsand

大都会：一段时间以来，大都会将是卖家已经很明显，但最大的问题是他们能交易哪些球员。先发投手 Freddy Peralta 和 Clay Holmes 很可能被交易，后援投手 A.J. Minter 和 Brooks Raley 也是如此。有趣的问题是，任何合同持续到2027年之后的球员是否可能被交易。大都会真的会交易 Francisco Lindor 吗？他有完全不可交易条款，所以他能掌握自己的命运。那 Francisco Alvarez 呢？目前，几乎任何事情都有可能。-- Mark Feinsand
国民：国民处于外卡竞争中，他们的策略取决于球队在8月3日前的表现。与过去五个赛季明确卖家不同，如果他们认为可以冲击季后赛，今年他们可能会买进。如果他们决定出售，游击手 CJ Abrams、一垒手 Luis Garcia Jr. 和左投手 Foster Griffin（合同即将到期）是主要交易候选人。-- Jessica Camerato
费城人：自 Don Mattingly 于4月28日成为临时经理以来，费城人一直是棒球界最好的球队之一，但他们有几个需求：牛棚、右打和五号先发。但他们也有棒球界最弱的农场系统之一，所以看他们能在质量和数量上做多少将很有趣。-- Todd Zolecki
国联中区

酿酒人：自2018年以来，他们几乎每年都是买家，今年也将是买家，但酿酒人很少有大动作。更多时候，他们明智地增加或出于深度考虑，就像2018年需要投手时交易 Jordan Lyles，或者2023年需要打者时交易 Carlos Santana 和 Mark Canha。这些动作并不总是有效——酿酒人在交易截止日的牛棚补强尤其令人担忧——但它们也不会在潜力新秀损失方面反咬一口。-- Adam McCalvy
红雀：圣路易斯是今年竞争中另一支惊喜球队，但棒球运营总裁 Chaim Bloom 不太可能成为大买家，无论红雀离季后赛席位多近。Bloom 可能寻求买卖兼顾，即将成为自由球员的 Dustin May 和 Lars Nootbar 是最合理的交易筹码。增加先发投手深度——最好是可控的投手——应该是重点。-- Mark Feinsand
小熊：小熊将是买家，截止日前的这段时期对于管理层决定在追求投手方面投入多大力度至关重要。棒球运营总裁 Jed Hoyer 将首轮轮空赔率作为积极性的指标。明确的是，芝加哥需要投手——轮值和牛棚——因为整个赛季都受到大量伤病影响。小熊将寻找以深度为名义的小动作，但预计北区人会考虑大动作。那可能是一名有影响力的先发投手或后段局数的后援投手，或者甚至两者都想要。-- Jordan Bastian
海盗：由于海盗处于国联外卡竞争激烈中，他们可能会积极寻找改善其明确需求领域的方法：牛棚。匹兹堡的进攻是棒球界最好的之一，布奇队的先发轮值正在改善，Paul Skenes、Braxton Ashcraft 和 Jared Jones 领衔。薪资对海盗来说总是一个问题，他们可能试图通过交易 Marcell Ozuna 或其他打者来腾出空间。-- Aiden Stepansky

红人：根据战绩，红人将是卖家。唯一的问题是，他们会走多远？他们有七名球员将在赛季末成为自由球员，其中没有人预计会在冬季与其他球队签约时获得合格报价。领先这一群体的是先发投手 Brady Singer、重炮手 Eugenio Suárez 和捕手 Tyler Stephenson。但在合同或球队控制期持续到2026年之后的球员中，棒球运营总裁 Nick Krall 可能会接到关于超级工具人 Spencer Steer 和先发投手 Hunter Greene 的电话。-- Mark Sheldon
国联西区

响尾蛇：除非未来10天崩溃，否则响尾蛇将在交易截止日成为买家。他们在未来几周内有一些投手回归，如先发投手 Michael Soroka、终结者 Justin Martinez，可能还有 Zac Gallen。其他投手，如先发 Corbin Burnes 和 Ryne Nelson，应该在十月准备好。鉴于这一点以及牛棚至今的表现，响尾蛇可能专注于增加一名打者，特别是一垒手，这个位置今年一直是他们的黑洞。-- Steve Gilbert
道奇：即使 Shohei Ohtani 的投球暂时搁置，Will Smith 的缺席时间比预期的更长，道奇更可能进行边缘补强，而不是任何重大动作——尽管如果合适的机会出现，他们永远不能被排除在外。洛杉矶有潜力新秀资本来完成大交易，但也可以利用交易筹码进行横向移动，进一步加强农场系统。-- Sonja Chen
巨人：作为少数确定的卖家之一，巨人将继续希望他们能摆脱一份大合同（Rafael Devers、Willy Adames、Matt Chapman、李政厚），尽管为这些球员找到匹配困难。不过，旧金山有两个有吸引力的交易筹码：二垒手 Luis Arraez 和左投手 Robbie Ray，两人都将在本赛季后成为自由球员。预计这两名租借球员都会被交易以获得可观回报。-- Mark Feinsand

教士：教士想要买进。但并不确定。他们处于外卡竞争之外，进攻本赛季大部分时间挣扎，轮值有重大疑问。他们可以在截止日解决这些问题。但前提是他们首先能证明自己是可行的竞争者。接下来10天至关重要。如果他们进一步落后，就有理由卖出并为未来带回一些价值。也有可能他们按兵不动。或者，他们可以混合搭配，在某些领域升级，同时交易其他领域。本月早些时候被问及他的路径时，总经理 A.J. Preller 指出：“所有这些选项都在桌面上。”-- AJ Cassavell

洛矶：周一的交易——将三A投手 Nick Frasso 和单A外野手 Landyn Vidourek 从道奇换来后援投手 Seth Halvorsen——揭示了部分策略。Frasso 预计很快升上大联盟，Vidourek 将被培养——而他们放弃了过去紧握的那种年轻强力投手。阵容中也遍布老将，因此先发投手 Tomoyuki Sugano 和 Michael Lorenzen、后援投手 Brennan Bernardino 和 Jimmy Herget，以及外野手 Jake McCarthy 和 Mickey Moniak 可能吸引其他球队。虽然洛矶预计会很活跃，但要他们放弃两届全明星捕手 Hunter Goodman，需要巨额回报。-- Thomas Harding
items.author: MLB.com

items.titleEn: Blue Jays hoping to rekindle offense, 'throw some uppercuts' as Deadline looms
bodyEn: TORONTO -- For the Blue Jays to get where they want to go, whether that’s in 2026 or beyond, finding some thump at the plate would go a long way toward easing their offensive struggles.

That search for runs and power continued for Toronto in Wednesday’s 4-2 loss to the Rays at Rogers Centre, as the Blue Jays managed just four hits and weren’t able to build any momentum after scoring in the first inning for the first time since July 12.

“[Tampa Bay’s] got some of the best arms in baseball, but it’s been a tough stretch for sure, kind of collectively up and down,” Blue Jays manager John Schneider said. “But hopefully, you make adjustments. You like the way [we] start today, but I think guys are grinding; guys are pressing. So, you combine that with good pitching, and that’s where we’re at.”

It’s no coincidence that both of the Blue Jays’ runs came in the innings in which they had extra-base hits. First, a double off the right-field wall from Vladimir Guerrero Jr. helped them push the game’s opening run across, moving Nathan Lukes from first to third before George Springer’s run-scoring groundout. Then, Alejandro Kirk’s solo homer in the seventh cut Toronto’s deficit to two -- but it couldn’t muster anything else from there.

Despite Guerrero Jr.’s 104.5 mph liner in the first, Tampa Bay’s pitching held the Blue Jays to just six hard-hit balls (with an exit velocity of 95 mph or higher), according to Statcast, only two of which went for hits.

Since the All-Star break, Toronto has been held to 10 runs over six games by the Rays and White Sox, going 1-5 in that stretch and falling to 10 games under .500 (46-56) with the Aug. 3 Trade Deadline on the horizon.
So, as the quiet offensive performances and the urgency that comes with this time of the season mount, how can the Blue Jays find a breakthrough offensively?

“I think you have to remind guys, 'It's OK [to put the ball in play], but you have to still take some shots, throw some uppercuts,'” Schneider said. “And for whatever reason, I feel like when you're not going well, it's safe just to say, ‘OK, I'm going to put the ball in play.’ And we've been trying to tell the guys, ‘You got to flip it. You got to be the other way around.’
“Until you get to two strikes, you gotta really be waiting for a pitch you can hit hard. And when we start doing that, it's a lot easier to score some runs.”

Ultimately, those bigger swings didn’t connect on Wednesday, and the Blue Jays couldn't make their two runs stand up in a game in which Tampa Bay scored three unearned runs in the sixth after Daulton Varsho dropped a Yandy Díaz fly ball at the track to lead off the frame.

The decisive inning followed a bounce-back showing from Spencer Miles, who was pitching behind an opener after surrendering six runs in four-plus innings on Friday against the White Sox. Miles worked 3 2/3 scoreless frames, needing 51 pitches to navigate the Rays’ lineup while striking out four and continuing his breakout rookie season.

“They don’t strike out a lot, so the fact I could get some swing-and-miss, even with my stuff ticked down a little bit tonight, showed I was pitching a little more,” said Miles, who generated six whiffs in his outing. “I’m getting ahead in counts and then putting them away, which I liked a ton.”

Miles’ role will continue to evolve as the season wears on. Whether he works mostly in a bulk-relief role or takes on more traditional starts will likely depend on what direction the Blue Jays decide to go at the upcoming Deadline.

But for a pitcher who entered 2026 with just 14 2/3 professional innings under his belt, each outing the rest of the way presents the 25-year-old with an opportunity to keep learning at the big league level.

“Sometimes you have it, and sometimes you don’t,” Miles said. “But knowing that you can go out there and make pitches coming off [Friday’s outing], and tonight, bouncing back, making a bunch of pitches and getting some strikeouts when they counted, I felt really good.”

As encouraging as Miles’ response was, until the Blue Jays find their way to some more consistent offense, they’ll continue to play these tight low-scoring games where the smallest mistakes can make all the difference.
items.titleZh: 蓝鸟希望重燃进攻，交易截止日前“挥出上勾拳”
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟进攻乏力，4-2负于光芒，期望在交易截止日前找回重击能力。
bodyZh: 多伦多——无论蓝鸟希望在2026年还是更远的未来达到目标，在打击区找到一些重击能力将极大缓解他们的进攻困境。
周三在罗杰斯中心以4-2输给光芒的比赛中，多伦多继续寻找得分和力量，蓝鸟仅击出4支安打，并且在自7月12日以来首次在第一局得分后未能建立任何势头。
“（坦帕湾）拥有棒球界最佳的一些投手，但这确实是一段艰难的时期，整体上起伏不定，”蓝鸟经理John Schneider说。“但希望你能做出调整。你喜欢我们今天开局的方式，但我认为球员们在挣扎；球员们在施压。所以，当你将其与出色的投球相结合时，这就是我们现在的处境。”
蓝鸟的两分都来自他们击出长打的局数，这并非巧合。首先，Vladimir Guerrero Jr.击出一支右外野墙边的二垒安打，帮助球队推进了本场比赛的第一分，将Nathan Lukes从一垒送到三垒，随后George Springer击出高飞牺牲打得分。然后，Alejandro Kirk在第七局击出 solo 本垒打，将多伦多的落后差距缩小到两分——但此后他们再无所获。
尽管Guerrero Jr.在第一局击出104.5英里的平飞球，根据Statcast，坦帕湾的投手将蓝鸟限制在只有6颗强力击球（出口速度95英里/小时或更高），其中只有两支成为安打。
自全明星赛以来，多伦多在六场比赛中被光芒和白袜限制在10分，期间战绩为1胜5负，随着8月3日交易截止日的临近，胜率跌至五成以下10场（46胜56负）。
那么，当进攻表现低迷以及赛季这个时期带来的紧迫感不断增加时，蓝鸟如何在进攻上取得突破？
“我认为你必须提醒球员们，‘把球打进场内没问题，但你仍然需要奋力一击，挥出上勾拳，’”Schneider说。“不管出于什么原因，我觉得当你状态不好时，简单地说‘好吧，我要把球打进场内’是很安全的。我们一直试图告诉球员们，‘你必须反过来想。你必须反过来。’
“直到你两好球之前，你真的要等待一个可以大力击打的球。当我们开始这样做时，得分就容易多了。”
最终，那些更大的挥杆在周三没有奏效，蓝鸟无法在坦帕湾在第六局得到三分非自责分的情况下守住两分的领先，当时Daulton Varsho在警戒区漏接了Yandy Díaz的飞球，开启了该局。
决定性的一局发生在Spencer Miles的反弹表现之后，他在周五对阵白袜的比赛中投了四局多失六分，这场担任开场投手后的长中继。Miles投了3 2/3局无失分，用了51球面对光芒打线，三振四人，延续了他突破性的新秀赛季。
“他们不太容易被三振，所以事实上我能取得一些挥空，即使今晚我的球威稍微下降，也表明我投得更聪明了一些，”Miles说，他在这场比赛中制造了六次挥空。“我在球数上领先，然后解决他们，我非常喜欢这一点。”
随着赛季的进行，Miles的角色将继续演变。他主要是担任大批量中继角色还是承担更多传统先发，可能取决于蓝鸟在即将到来的交易截止日决定的方向。
但对于一位进入2026年时仅有14 2/3局职业投球经验的投手来说，余下每次出赛都让这位25岁的球员有机会在大联盟层面继续学习。
“有时你状态好，有时不好，”Miles说。“但知道我可以上场投球，并在（周五的）比赛后反弹，今晚做出调整，投出很多好球并在关键时刻取得一些三振，我感觉非常好。”
尽管Miles的回应令人鼓舞，但在蓝鸟找到更稳定的进攻之前，他们将继续打这些紧张的低比分比赛，最小的失误都可能产生决定性的影响。
items.author: Zach Worden

items.titleEn: Blue Jays' No. 2 prospect Nimmala proving he belongs -- and flexing as well -- at Double-A
bodyEn: The trajectories for prospects are rarely linear ... just ask Arjun Nimmala.

MLB's No. 44 prospect has had moments that hinted at stardom and others that served as reminders that development is anything but a straight line. Now in his fourth professional season, Nimmala is beginning to put it all together and turning tantalizing potential into consistent production.
Nimmala homered for the third time in seven games and finished with a pair of knocks to help lead Double-A New Hampshire past visiting Somerset, 7-4, on Wednesday night at Delta Dental Stadium.
Get tickets to a Minor League game near you »

The Blue Jays' No. 2 prospect has hit safely in six of seven games for the Fisher Cats, going 6-for-25 with four extra-base hits and three RBIs. Prior to his activation from the 7-day IL on July 7, Nimmala had not appeared in a game at Double-A since he strained his hamstring on May 27, which landed him on the IL three days later.

While he hasn't been tearing the cover off the ball since his return, the native of Tampa, Florida, has been able to flex his muscles. Nimmala's third homer for New Hampshire was a no-doubter to the opposite field, landing an estimated 432 feet over the fence in right-center field.

The 6-foot, 190-pounder's third long ball in 22 plate appearances has reinforced the notion he's not only fully healthy but also comfortable in the Eastern League. Although he didn't go deep in his first 18 games on the circuit, Nimmala was thriving in other offensive facets of his game, hitting .308 with a .375 OBP.

That production came on the heels of a strong start to 2026 at High-A Vancouver, where he spent all of last season. Nimmala was one of the younger players in the Northwest League last season, and it showed at times, particularly late in the season when he faded after a strong April and May.

While Nimmala finished with respectable power numbers -- 45 extra-base hits, 13 homers, 70 runs and 61 RBIs in 120 games for the Canadians -- his struggles during the summer months prompted Toronto to keep him at the level to open this season. He responded positively, posting a .845 OPS and driving in 20 runs across 24 games.
Subscribe to the MLB Pipeline Newsletter

Nimmala's recent power surge has only added to his impressive season totals, despite his absence due to injury. The 20th overall pick in 2023 is still just 20 years old -- nearly four years younger than the average player in the Eastern League -- but his .829 OPS and nine extra-base hits in 25 contests are speaking volumes.
If his latest stretch is any indication, the consistency that once seemed just out of reach may finally be in the grasp of one of the game's brightest young talents.
items.titleZh: 蓝鸟二号新秀Nimmala在2A证明自己，同时展现力量
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟二号新秀Nimmala在2A级比赛中表现出色，包括本垒打。
bodyZh: 潜力新秀的成长轨迹很少是线性的……问问Arjun Nimmala就知道了。
MLB第44号潜力新秀有过暗示巨星潜质的时刻，也有过提醒人们发展绝非一帆风顺的瞬间。现在，在他的第四个职业赛季中，Nimmala开始将所有因素整合起来，将诱人的潜力转化为持续的输出。
周三晚在Delta Dental体育场，Nimmala在七场比赛中第三次轰出本垒打，并有两支安打，帮助2A级新罕布什尔渔猫以7-4击败来访的萨默塞特爱国者。
获取附近小联盟比赛门票»
蓝鸟队的二号新秀在渔猫队的七场比赛中六场有安打，25打数6安打，包括四支长打和三分打点。在7月7日从7天伤病名单激活之前，Nimmala自5月27日腿筋拉伤后（三天后进入伤病名单）未在2A级比赛中出场。
虽然复出后并未完全爆发，但这位来自佛罗里达州坦帕的球员已经能够展示他的力量。Nimmala在渔猫队的第三支本垒打是一支毫无悬念的反方向本垒打，估计飞行距离432英尺，越过右中墙。
这位6英尺、190磅的球员在22次出场中轰出第三支长打，强化了他不仅完全健康而且在东区联盟中游刃有余的观点。尽管在该联盟的前18场比赛中没有开轰，但Nimmala在其他进攻方面表现出色，打击率.308，上垒率.375。
这一表现是在2026赛季于高A级温哥华加拿大人队强势开局之后取得的，他去年整个赛季都在那里。Nimmala上赛季是西北联盟中最年轻的球员之一，这在某些时候有所体现，尤其是在四五月强势表现后于赛季末段下滑。
虽然Nimmala最终打出了可观的 power 数据——在为加拿大人队出战的120场比赛中，有45支长打、13支本垒打、70分得分和61分打点——但他在夏季月份的挣扎促使多伦多让他本赛季从该级别开始。他做出了积极回应，在24场比赛中打出.845的OPS，并贡献20分打点。
订阅MLB Pipeline新闻通讯
尽管因伤缺席，Nimmala近期的本垒打飙升进一步提升了其令人印象深刻的赛季总数据。2023年选秀第20顺位的他仍只有20岁——比东区联盟平均年龄小近四岁——但在25场比赛中.829的OPS和9支长打已经说明了很多。
如果最近的表现有所预示，那么一度看似难以企及的稳定性可能终于掌握在这位棒球界最耀眼的新星之一手中。
items.author: Michael Avallone

items.titleEn: Scherzer mirroring peak Mad Max in latest rehab start with five no-hit innings
bodyEn: TORONTO -- Despite having his current rehab stint paused after needing three injections in his right thumb over the All-Star break, Blue Jays right-hander Max Scherzer delivered a vintage performance in his return to the mound with Triple-A Buffalo.

Scherzer made his third rehab start of his current ramp-up on Wednesday for the Bisons, continuing to trend back to Toronto’s Major League rotation after hitting the injured list with a back injury in mid-June.
Here’s everything you need to know about Scherzer’s afternoon in Buffalo:
How did he perform?

Scherzer was as sharp as we’ve seen him this season, working five scoreless and hitless innings for the Bisons while striking out seven against the Rochester Red Wings. The 41-year-old threw 40 of his 56 pitches for strikes and generated 11 whiffs on 33 swings.
The only baserunner he allowed came on an error in the third inning.
A three-time Cy Young winner, Scherzer mixed in 28 four-seamers, 14 sliders, nine changeups and five curveballs in the outing while averaging 92.6 mph with his fastball.
In his two previous starts on this rehab assignment -- one with Buffalo and one with High-A Vancouver -- Scherzer surrendered seven runs (six earned) over seven innings and struck out eight.
What’s next?
After the pause coming out of the break, Scherzer will likely need another rehab start to increase his pitch count beyond the 60-pitch threshold before returning to the Blue Jays.

“Ideally it’s more,” Toronto manager John Schneider said Wednesday. “You tax the bullpen, even if he’s throwing well. So, I think ideally he’s built up a little bit more. I don’t want to go back and forth with it. I kind of want to just know that’s at least 80.”

In the meantime, the Blue Jays will continue to rely upon breakout Rule 5 Draft selection Spencer Miles to handle the bulk duties in a bullpen day to make it through the No. 5 starter’s turn through the rotation.
“I have a pretty good feel that I’m going to recover pretty good from this one,” Scherzer said in Buffalo. “This wasn’t a taxing outing. Arm felt great, body felt great, thumb felt great.”
Scherzer has thrown just 22 MLB innings for the Blue Jays this season across six starts and owns a 10.23 ERA.
What are they saying?
“It looked like Max,” Schneider said. “The big thing was holding velo, which I think he did. That’s what he was focused on today. So, [we’ll] see what he says when he gets here.”

“For me, you’re just looking at the process of executing pitches,” Scherzer said after the outing. “It’s been two weeks, so to be able to get back into the zone and be able to navigate a lineup and execute pitches, that’s the good. Whether you give up hits or not, for me, I’m just focused on the process and execution.”
What else?

With the Blue Jays dropping to nine games under .500 in Tuesday’s 12-2 loss to the Rays, Scherzer’s pending return could ultimately prove to be too little, too late for Toronto if things continue to trend in the wrong direction before the Aug. 3 Trade Deadline.

“I just gotta worry about myself. I gotta worry about my health and going out there and being the best that I can be,” Scherzer said when asked if there was an urgency to get back to the Blue Jays. “I can’t make the team win. I can only go out there and compete and do my job to put the team in a position to win.”
As for the Bisons, they were unable to hold onto the shutout bid after Scherzer’s scoreless start, allowing a home run in the seventh.
items.titleZh: 薛泽复出小联盟先发五局无安打
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟右投Max Scherzer在3A小联盟复出先发五局无安打，送出7次三振。
bodyZh: 多伦多——尽管Max Scherzer的全明星周末期间因右手拇指需要三次注射导致当前复健进程暂停，这位蓝鸟右投在重返3A水牛城野牛队投手丘时交出了复古级表现。
周三，Scherzer为野牛队进行了本次复健计划中的第三场先发，自六月中旬因背伤进入伤病名单后，他持续恢复向多伦多大联盟轮值回归。
以下是关于Scherzer在水牛城下午表现你需要了解的一切：
他表现如何？
Scherzer本赛季状态最佳，为野牛队主投五局无失分且未被击出安打，面对罗彻斯特红翼队送出7次三振。这位41岁投手56球中有40球好球，33次挥棒中制造11次挥空。
他唯一允许的上垒是第三局的一次失误。
这位三届赛扬奖得主此役混合使用了28颗四缝线速球、14颗滑球、9颗变速球和5颗曲球，速球平均时速92.6英里。
在本次复健任务的前两次先发（一次为水牛城，一次为高阶1A温哥华）中，Scherzer在7局中失7分（6分自责），送出8次三振。
接下来是什么？
在休息日暂停后，Scherzer可能还需要一次复健先发，将用球数提高到60球以上，才能回归蓝鸟。
“理想情况是更多，”蓝鸟总教练John Schneider周三表示。“即使他投得很好，也会消耗牛棚。所以我认为理想情况是他再增加一些球数。我不想来回折腾，我想至少知道有80球。”
与此同时，蓝鸟将继续依赖规则5选秀的惊喜Spencer Miles在牛棚日承担大量局数，以度过第五号先发轮值。
“我很有把握这次恢复会很好，”Scherzer在水牛城表示。“这不是一次消耗很大的出赛。手臂感觉很好，身体感觉很好，拇指感觉很好。”
Scherzer本赛季为蓝鸟在大联盟先发6场仅投22局，自责分率高达10.23。
他们在说什么？
“看起来像是Max，”Schneider表示。“重点是保持球速，我认为他做到了。这是他今天专注的事情。所以，我们会看看他回来后会怎么说。”
“对我来说，我只是看执行投球的过程，”Scherzer赛后说道。“已经过了两周，所以能够回到好球带，能够对付打线并执行投球，这是好事。无论是否被击出安打，对我来说，我只关注过程和执行。”
还有呢？
随着蓝鸟周二12-2输给光芒后胜率跌破五成九场，如果情况在8月3日交易截止日前继续恶化，Scherzer即将回归可能最终对多伦多来说太少太晚。
“我只需要担心自己。我只需要担心我的健康，上场做到最好，”当被问及是否有紧迫感回到蓝鸟时，Scherzer表示。“我不能让球队赢球。我只能上场竞争，做好我的工作，让球队处于赢球位置。”
至于野牛队，在Scherzer无失分先发后，他们未能保住完封，第七局被击出本垒打。
items.author: Zach Worden

items.titleEn: Key takeaways: Rays 12, Blue Jays 2
bodyEn: TORONTO -- These past two days, the Rays have looked like a team specifically designed to expose all of the Blue Jays’ flaws.

Tuesday’s scoreboard showed a 12-2 loss for the Blue Jays, but this felt like too many days they have had recently, all of them blurring into one another as the last Wild Card spot drifts further away.
This is what mattered most from Tuesday:
1. One eye on the Wild Card standings
Well, maybe both eyes at this point.

With the Orioles-Red Sox game postponed by heavy rain in Boston, the Blue Jays’ loss drops them to six games back of that final Wild Card spot. They’re chasing the hottest team in baseball in the Red Sox, though, who have won 14 games in a row and sit just one win shy of tying their franchise record. Five teams sit between the Blue Jays and Red Sox, too. There’s no good news to be found on MLB.com’s Wild Card standings, even if you can’t stop checking it.

This is all moving in the wrong direction right now. There are 11 games between now and the Deadline on Aug. 3, and at this point, the Blue Jays need more than just some series wins. This club’s only option is to do what the Red Sox have done -- or at least a good chunk of that -- to save themselves from selling.
2. Where is Daulton Varsho’s power?
Let’s go back to Spring Training for a moment, back before this strange, frustrating season began.

Daulton Varsho, fresh off hitting 20 homers in just 71 games in his injury-shortened 2025, looked fantastic. Sure, it’s just Spring Training, but Varsho looked like a hitter primed to have a massive season heading into free agency. That hasn’t happened, though, and Varsho has just seven homers with a .684 OPS through these first 101 games after chipping in an RBI single Tuesday.

Strength and talent aren’t the issues here. While not the biggest athlete on the team, Varsho is built for power and can put on a show in batting practice when he wants to. This is more about Varsho’s mental approach, and at this point, his manager wants to see him loosen up. Another hitter chasing singles won’t help this lineup.

“Let it rip. You don’t have to hit .280 to be productive. He showed that last year,” manager John Schneider said. “I get where everyone is in their careers, and you want to have your numbers be a certain thing. I’m just reminding him that his value is in that. You need to hit some balls in the seats and you need to play good defense.”

Varsho came to the plate in a big spot in the first inning, with the bases loaded and just one out, but hit a soft pop fly to third baseman Junior Caminero. The big moments seem to keep finding Varsho, but it’s been nearly a month since his last home run (June 23).
3. What can Schneider do?
The manager made it clear Monday that even if everyone wants him to get red in the face and call out his team, that’s not happening. His job, for now, is to be the steady voice at the top.
What else can Schneider do, though? Are there any other lineups he can try, beyond drawing names from a hat? Tuesday’s version of trying something new was batting Ernie Clement third.

“Trying something different, trying to get some contact with some guys on base,” Schneider said. “He’s been one of our best hitters, and we’re not scoring, so we’re changing it up a little bit and we’ll see how it goes.”

This is where the Blue Jays are at right now, and frankly, these “let’s just see what happens” changes are all that’s left. They’ve used up all of the “good” ideas -- or at least all of the obvious ones -- and are left to hope that something sticks.
items.titleZh: 关键要点：光芒12-2蓝鸟
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟遭遇惨败，外卡席位渐行渐远，瓦肖火力不足成隐忧。
bodyZh: 多伦多——过去两天，光芒看起来像一支专门设计来暴露蓝鸟所有缺陷的球队。
周二的比分板显示蓝鸟以2-12落败，但这感觉像是他们最近经历了许多这样的日子，所有这些都模糊成一片，因为最后一张外卡席位越来越远。
以下是周二最重要的事情：
1. 紧盯外卡排名
嗯，到了这个地步，也许两只眼睛都要盯着。
由于金莺与红袜的比赛因波士顿暴雨推迟，蓝鸟的失利使他们落后最后一张外卡席位六场。不过，他们追赶的是红袜，这支目前大联盟最火热的球队，已经取得14连胜，距离追平队史纪录仅差一场胜利。而且，蓝鸟与红袜之间还隔着五支球队。即使你忍不住去查看，在MLB.com的外卡排名上也找不到任何好消息。
目前一切都在朝着错误的方向发展。从现在到8月3日的交易截止日还有11场比赛，而蓝鸟需要的不仅仅是赢得一些系列赛。这支球队唯一的选择就是做红袜所做的事——或者至少是其中很大一部分——来避免成为卖家。
2. Daulton Varsho的火力去哪了？
让我们暂时回到春训，回到这个奇怪而令人沮丧的赛季开始之前。
Daulton Varsho在2025年因伤缩短的赛季中，仅用71场比赛就击出20支本垒打，状态看起来棒极了。当然，这只是春训，但Varsho看起来像是一位准备在进入自由市场前打出爆发赛季的击球员。然而，这并没有发生，Varsho在周二贡献了一分打点的一垒安打后，本赛季前101场比赛仅击出7支本垒打，OPS为.684。
力量和天赋并非问题所在。虽然Varsho并非队中最强壮的运动员，但他具备力量型身材，并能在打击练习中根据需要展现技巧。这更多是关于Varsho的心理状态，而此时此刻，他的总教练希望他能放松下来。另一个追求一垒安打的击球员无助于提升打线。
"放手一搏。你不需要打击率.280才能有贡献。他去年就证明了这一点。"总教练John Schneider说道。"我理解每个人所处的职业生涯阶段，都希望自己的数据达到一定水平。我只是提醒他，他的价值就在于此。你需要把一些球轰上看台，还需要做好防守。"
Varsho在第一局的大好局面下上场打击，当时满垒且只有一人出局，但他击出了一个软弱的内野高飞球，被三垒手Junior Caminero接杀。关键时刻似乎总是找上Varsho，但他距离上一次本垒打（6月23日）已经过去将近一个月。
3. Schneider能做什么？
总教练周一明确表示，即使每个人都希望他涨红脸并斥责全队，他也不会这么做。目前，他的工作就是作为球队稳定声音的来源。
但Schneider还能做什么呢？除了从帽子里抽名字之外，他还能尝试其他阵容吗？周二尝试新方法是将Ernie Clement排在了第三棒。
"尝试一些不同的东西，试图在有跑者上垒时制造些安打，"Schneider说道。"他一直是我们的最佳击球员之一，而我们得分不多，所以我们做了一些调整，看看效果如何。"
这就是蓝鸟目前的处境，坦白说，这些"我们等着看会发生什么"的改变已经是仅剩的选择。他们已经用尽了所有"好"主意——或者至少是所有显而易见的主意——只能寄希望于某件事能奏效。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: Gausman not thinking about Trade Deadline after latest rough outing
bodyEn: TORONTO -- At this point, every single thing lives in the light of the Trade Deadline.

Each loss is another step closer to the Blue Jays, at some point in the days leading up to Aug. 3, announcing to the rest of baseball that they’re sellers. It’s baseball’s version of a concession speech. When done right, it can soften the blow and bring a relieving end to the inevitable, but that’s about it.

So many Blue Jays players, too, will be viewed through this lens over the next 11 games. If Kevin Gausman’s name hits the trade market between now and then, Tuesday’s start in a 12-2 loss could be one of his last for Toronto, perhaps even his final at home. Even now, when everything is still theoretical, that just doesn’t sound right. Gausman, in his 4 1/2 years here, has been everything the Blue Jays dreamed of and more.

"I'm not going to think about that too much. I'm a Blue Jay right now, and the focus is this team, this room and those guys,” Gausman said. “That's where my mind is at right now. That is kind of crazy to think about."

Gausman is 35 now. He’s lived so many lives in this game, first as the fourth overall pick out of LSU and a top prospect for the Orioles. Then came the bad times, a trade to Atlanta, a move to the bullpen and even Gausman being designated for assignment. Fans in Toronto have known Gausman only at his best, but this has been a long road. Only six active pitchers have thrown more innings than his 2,026 2/3: Justin Verlander (3,571 1/3), Max Scherzer (2,985), Chris Sale (2,189), Jose Quintana (2,142 1/3) and Patrick Corbin (2,127 2/3).
Gausman knows how this game works by now -- both on and off the field.

“It’s impossible to ignore nowadays with social media and the amount of people that are here right now,” Gausman said after Tuesday’s loss. “We kind of all know what’s going on. Unfortunately, we knew we needed to come out of the [second-half] gate strong, and we really haven’t. We just haven’t played very well lately.”

Trading relievers on one-year deals and veteran role players? That’s the easy part, at least relatively speaking. Gausman is stitched into the fabric of this organization now, someone who will likely be back in town five, 10 and 20 years from now as an alumnus. He and his family have embraced this city as their home, so this isn’t as simple as cutting a long hotel reservation a month or two short.
Manager John Schneider doesn’t want to touch Trade Deadline talk until he needs to, even as it inches nearer. That may be the only way to stay sane in his job.

“I’m not going there. That’s a slippery slope. That’s above my pay grade,” Schneider said after Monday’s loss. “These guys all know what the standings are. They all know who we’re playing tomorrow. We all expected that we’d be in a different position right now, but there’s nothing you can do about what’s already been done.”

The Blue Jays are six games back of the last Wild Card spot now, though, chasing a Red Sox team that’s won 14 in a row, one shy of tying their franchise record. There are five teams in between Toronto and that final spot, too, so there’s nothing simple about this. Each bad day, or even each day where these standings remain the same, pushes the Blue Jays closer to some of these uncomfortable decisions.

Shane Bieber could be attractive to contenders, especially with another strong start after his last one. Daulton Varsho, even in a down year, brings speed, defense and the potential that his bat comes alive. George Springer could be a veteran addition with postseason experience. Jeff Hoffman, even with a year left on his deal, could be awfully attractive to contenders.
“I don’t want to look at the big picture until we need to look at the big picture,” Schneider reiterated Monday, and can you blame him?

It’s just getting bigger and louder, though. Anything outside of a string of wins will continue to feed this tension, which you could feel in the air at Rogers Centre the moment the Rays took an early lead again on Tuesday night.
It’s a sense of approaching dread, even for those holding out hope that the Blue Jays can shock the baseball world and pull this back out of the ditch.
items.titleZh: 高斯曼近期表现不佳后不愿考虑交易截止日
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟遭遇惨败，高斯曼坦言目前只关注球队表现。
bodyZh: 多伦多——在这个节骨眼上，每一件事都笼罩在交易截止日的光环之下。
每一次失利都让蓝鸟向卖家身份靠近一步，在8月3日之前的某个时刻，他们终将向全联盟宣告自己成为卖方。这是棒球界的认输演讲。如果做得好，它能减缓打击，给不可避免的结局带来解脱，但也仅此而已。
同样，在未来的11场比赛中，许多蓝鸟球员也将被放在这面放大镜下审视。如果凯文·高斯曼的名字从现在到那时出现在交易市场上，那么周二12比2失利的先发可能是他在多伦多的最后一场之一，甚至可能是他在主场的最后一战。即便现在一切都还是理论上的，这听起来就不对劲。高斯曼在这里的四年半里，一直是蓝鸟梦寐以求的一切，甚至更多。
“我不会想太多。我现在是蓝鸟的一员，重点是这个球队、这个房间和那些家伙，”高斯曼说。“这就是我现在的想法。想到那个有点疯狂。”

高斯曼现在35岁了。他在这项运动中经历过太多角色：最初是路易斯安那州立大学的第四顺位新秀，金莺队的顶级新秀。然后经历了低谷，被交易到亚特兰大，转入牛棚，甚至被指定转让。多伦多的球迷只见过最好的高斯曼，但这是一条漫长的道路。现役投手中，只有六位投球局数超过他的2026又2/3局：贾斯汀·维兰德（3571又1/3局）、马克斯·谢泽尔（2985局）、克里斯·塞尔（2189局）、何塞·金塔纳（2142又1/3局）和帕特里克·科尔宾（2127又2/3局）。
高斯曼现在深知这项运动在场上和场下是如何运作的。
“如今在社交媒体的环境下，以及现在这里的人数，想忽略是不可能的，”高斯曼在周二失利后说。“我们基本上都知道发生了什么。不幸的是，我们知道自己需要在后半程开局打出强势表现，但我们并没有。我们最近打得真的很不好。”
交易一年合同的牛棚投手和老将角色球员？这还算简单，至少相对而言。高斯曼已经融入这支球队的血脉，他很可能在五年、十年、二十年后以校友身份回到这里。他和家人已经把这座城市当成了家，所以这可不是提前一两周缩短酒店预订那么简单。
主教练约翰·施耐德在不得不谈论之前不想触碰交易截止日的话题，即使它越来越近。这或许是他在工作中保持理智的唯一方式。
“我不谈那个。那是个滑坡。那超出了我的职责范围，”施耐德在周一失利后说。“这些家伙都知道排名。他们都知道明天要对阵谁。我们都期望现在能处于不同的位置，但已经发生的事情无法改变。”
不过，蓝鸟现在落后最后一个外卡席位六个胜场，要追赶的是一支已经14连胜的红袜队，后者距离追平队史纪录只差一场。多伦多和那个席位之间还有五支球队，所以事情并不简单。每一个糟糕的日子，甚至排名没有变化的每一天，都在推动蓝鸟接近这些令人不安的决定。
肖恩·比伯可能对竞争者很有吸引力，尤其是在上一次出色先发后再来一次强势表现。道尔顿·瓦肖即使处于低迷赛季，也能提供速度、防守和打擊复苏的潜力。乔治·斯普林格可能成为一位有季后赛经验的老将补强。杰夫·霍夫曼尽管还有一年合同，但对竞争者可能非常有吸引力。
“在必须考虑大局之前，我不想看大局，”施耐德周一重申，你能责怪他吗？
但这个问题越来越明显，越来越响亮。任何非连胜的表现都会继续助长这种紧张气氛，周二晚上当光芒队再次早早领先时，罗杰斯中心的气氛就能感受到这一点。
这是一种迫近的恐惧感，即使对于那些仍抱有希望蓝鸟能震惊棒球界、将这辆车从沟里拉出来的人来说也是如此。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: LHP Cole Carlon could blaze a path to The Show
bodyEn: Now that the 2026 Draft is in the books, the question becomes: When will we see these players in the big leagues?
Timelines to the Majors can vary. No one from the 2025 class has reached The Show yet, but six players from the 2024 Draft debuted there before the first anniversary of their selection.

On the latest episode of the MLB Pipeline Podcast, Jim Callis and Jonathan Mayo alternated picking four recent draftees whom they thought would be the fastest to the Majors. After all, what's more fitting than a draft of a Draft?
Cole Carlon, LHP, Blue JaysPick: No. 39 overall (Draft No. 26)

The skinny: The Arizona native was the second-ranked college lefty in the class thanks to a double-plus slider and his workhorse 6-foot-5 frame. Carlon only transitioned to starting this spring but had an excellent junior season.

Mayo: "He will be developed as a starter, there's no doubt in my mind, but I expect the Blue Jays to be able to hit the reset. This, after all, was a team that was inches away from winning the World Series last year. His 70-grade slider may be the best slider in the entire class. It's an 87-88 mph short, hard, cutter-like slider. He throws it a lot. He can manipulate it. Let's say he's in Double-A in the second half of the year. They can shorten him up, and that slider alone will get big league hitters out out of the bullpen."
Jackson Flora, RHP, GiantsPick: No. 4 overall (Draft No. 4)

The skinny: Flora is the latest potential ace out of UC Santa Barbara, following former teammate Tyler Bremner, who went second overall last year. At 6-foot-5 with impressive control and a fastball that touches triple digits, Flora is the top pitching prospect in the 2026 class.

Callis: "He's got the complete package. You've got an overwhelming fastball that he commands well. He's got a slider and a recently added kick-change that missed bats when guys try to sit on the heater. He throws plenty of strikes. He's got a durable build. The Giants need pitching."
Roch Cholowsky, SS, White SoxPick: No. 1 overall (Draft No. 2)

The skinny: Cholowsky is the top college shortstop prospect in more than two decades thanks to plus-or-better tools across the board, save for 45-grade speed. The right-handed hitter has a keen eye at the plate with many avenues to success in the big leagues.

Mayo: "There might be the possibility that he makes an appearance in the Arizona Fall League, which would be exciting for all of us. That would put him on a path to start the year in Double-A. I don't think he's up by late April, but he could be up by June. He's the one guy on this list that I could see being pre-All-Star break. Now if I had to pick a date, I probably would still pick the second half to err on the side of caution, but I do think that the path is there for him to get to Chicago ahead of the All-Star break."
Drew Burress, OF, AthleticsPick: No. 8 overall (Draft No. 7)

The skinny: Burress burst onto the scene at Georgia Tech by homering nine times in his first seven games and has continued producing at a high level. The center fielder can tap into impressive power from an open stance and has a disciplined approach.

Callis: "Drew Burress' flaw, as it were, is that he's 5-foot-9 and hits right-handed -- he's not bigger and hits left-handed. But in terms of tools, they're solid-to-plus across the board. I just think this guy is going to hit the ground running in the Minors, and I'm not saying he has Nick Kurtz's bat, but Jacob Wilson got to the big leagues very quickly. Nick Kurtz got to the big leagues very quickly. I don't think they're going to be very afraid to get Drew Burress to the big leagues very quickly either if his bat proves that it's ready."
Cal Randall, RHP, CardinalsPick: No. 146 overall (Draft No. 218)

The skinny: Randall found plenty of success as a reliever at UCLA thanks to a dominant fastball that played up because of his low release and extension that creates interesting angles. He has a little-used slider-cutter hybrid as well but throws his heater 89 percent of the time -- with a 36 percent whiff rate.

Mayo: "Randall is really interesting because he is all fastball. It sits 97 mph. It flirts with triple digits. He's obviously going to have to find a more consistently effective secondary pitch, and he's going to have to throw a few more strikes, but that fastball alone is a ticket to a fast track. Maybe you start him in Double-A, and then if he's dominating, you do not waste any time, and I think could very much see him impacting the Cardinals' bullpen next year."
Grady Emerson, SS, RaysPick: No. 2 overall (Draft No. 1)

The skinny: Konnor Griffin, the top high schooler drafted in 2024, made his MLB debut after just 127 Minor League games. Could Emerson, the first prep player taken in 2026, move that quickly? The Texas native has a 65-grade hit tool from the left side and virtually no flaws in his game.

Callis: "I do think when you have an elite-talent high school player, those guys can move very quickly. I think he's the best hitter in the entire Draft. I think he's an advanced defender. I think there's going to be power, there's gonna be speed. I just think the guys who are potential superstars move at their own timetable. He'd probably be more early 2028, but I do think he's that kind of talent who could just come out, tear up pro ball and really push the envelope as to when the Rays bring him to the big leagues."
Subscribe to the MLB Pipeline Newsletter
Jarren Advincula, 2B, AngelsPick: No. 45 overall (Draft No. 48)

The skinny: The Angels have earned a reputation for moving their prospects quickly, and first-rounder Jared Grindlinger -- a two-way prep prospect who's one of the youngest players in the Draft -- is a bit far away. But Advincula is an advanced college bat who won the Cape Cod League batting title after his freshman year at California and went on to hit .434 as a junior at Georgia Tech.

Mayo: "He's one of the best contact hitters in the entire class. There's not a ton of impact, but he never strikes out. He's got an incredible approach at the plate, there's enough pop for him to not have the bat knocked out of his hand, so I think it's going to transfer. He showed he can hit with wood, so I think he's going to hit, and he can run."
Taylor Rabe, RHP, RoyalsPick: No. 30 overall (Draft No. 40)

The skinny: Rabe is an athletic 6-foot-5 righty with one of the best fastballs in his class. Another point in his favor is his control, as he ranked fourth in NCAA Division I with a 7.0 K/BB ratio. The South Carolina native worked back from 2024 Tommy John surgery, and after beginning the season as a piggyback starter, developed into the No. 1 starter on a Mississippi team that made it to the Men's College World Series.

Callis: "The Royals really need pitching. So when he's ready, he's going to get the opportunity. He had some of the best combination of stuff and strikes in all of college baseball. He's up to 100 [mph] with really good command of his fastball. His slider and cutter came on late in the year. So I think this is a perfect convergence of talent and opportunity in Kansas City for Taylor Rabe."
items.titleZh: 左投Cole Carlon有望快速升上大联盟
bodyZh: 随着2026年选秀结束，问题变成：我们何时能看到这些球员登上大联盟？
登上大联盟的时间表各不相同。2025届还没有人升上大联盟，但2024届有六名球员在选秀一周年之前就已在大联盟亮相。
在最新一期MLB Pipeline播客中，Jim Callis和Jonathan Mayo轮流挑选了四位他们认为能最快升上大联盟的近期选秀球员。毕竟，还有什么比选秀中的选秀更合适的呢？
Cole Carlon, 左投, 蓝鸟 选秀顺位：第39顺位（选秀第26顺位）
简评：这位亚利桑那本地人凭借超强滑球和6英尺5英寸的身板成为本届第二号大学左投。Carlon今年春天才转为先发，但大三赛季表现出色。
Mayo：“毫无疑问球队会将他作为先发培养，但我预计蓝鸟会按下重置键。毕竟，这支球队去年距离赢得世界大赛只差一步。他的70分滑球可能是整个选秀中最好的滑球。这是一种87-88英里、短而硬的切球式滑球。他投得很多，且能操控球路。假设他今年下半季在2A，他们可以缩短他的投球局数，仅凭那颗滑球就能让大联盟打者出局。”
Jackson Flora, 右投, 巨人 选秀顺位：第4顺位（选秀第4顺位）
简评：Flora是加州大学圣塔芭芭拉分校最新出品的潜在王牌，此前他的队友Tyler Bremner在去年以第二顺位被选中。身高6英尺5英寸，控球出色，速球能触及三位数，Flora是2026届头号投手新秀。
Callis：“他拥有全套武器。他有威力十足的速球，且控球出色。他有滑球和最近增加的变速球，当打者试图等速球时，变速球能制造挥空。他投出大量好球。体格耐用。巨人需要投手。”
Roch Cholowsky, 游击, 白袜 选秀顺位：第1顺位（选秀第2顺位）
简评：Cholowsky是二十多年来最好的大学游击新秀，因为他几乎所有工具都在平均以上或以上，除了45分的速度。这位右打者选球敏锐，在大联盟有多种成功途径。
Mayo：“他有可能在亚利桑那秋季联盟亮相，这对我们所有人来说都令人兴奋。那将让他走上明年从2A开始的路径。我不认为他会在4月底前升上大联盟，但可能在6月前。他是这份名单上我认为可能在明星赛前就升上大联盟的球员。如果非要选一个时间，为谨慎起见我仍会选择下半季，但我确实认为他有在明星赛前就到达芝加哥的路径。”
Drew Burress, 外野, 运动家 选秀顺位：第8顺位（选秀第7顺位）
简评：Burress在佐治亚理工大学一鸣惊人，前七场比赛轰出九支本垒打，并持续高水平产出。这位中外野手能从开放式站姿击出惊人的力量，且打击纪律严明。
Callis：“Drew Burress的缺陷在于他身高5英尺9英寸且是右打——他不是更高大且左打。但就工具而言，他全面达到了平均以上。我认为这小伙子会在小联盟迅速起步，我不是说他有Nick Kurtz的打击能力，但Jacob Wilson升上大联盟很快。Nick Kurtz升上大联盟也很快。如果他的打击证明准备好了，我认为他们不会害怕让Drew Burress也快速升上大联盟。”
Cal Randall, 右投, 红雀 选秀顺位：第146顺位（选秀第218顺位）
简评：Randall在UCLA作为后援投手取得了不少成功，他依靠的是主导性的速球，由于低出手点和延伸创造出的角度，速球效果更佳。他还有极少使用的滑球-切球混合球，但89%的时间投速球——挥空率36%。
Mayo：“Randall非常有趣，因为他全是速球。速球时速97英里，偶尔触及三位数。显然他需要找到更有效的第二球种，也需要投更多好球，但仅凭那颗速球就足以走上快车道。也许让他在2A起步，如果他投出统治力，就别浪费时间，我认为他明年很可能影响红雀的牛棚。”
Grady Emerson, 游击, 光芒 选秀顺位：第2顺位（选秀第1顺位）
简评：Konnor Griffin，2024年选秀中第一位被选中的高中生，仅用了127场小联盟比赛就完成了大联盟首秀。Emerson，2026年第一位被选中的高中球员，能这么快吗？这位德州左打者拥有65分的击球工具，比赛中几乎没有任何缺陷。
Callis：“我认为当你拥有天赋异禀的高中生球员时，他们可以提升得非常快。我认为他是整个选秀中最好的击球手。我认为他是防守出色的球员。我认为他会有力量，有速度。我只是认为那些潜在的超级巨星有自己的时间表。他可能更早地在2028年，但我确实认为他有那种天赋，可以横空出世，在小联盟大杀四方，并真正推动光芒将他升上大联盟的时间表。”
订阅MLB Pipeline通讯
Jarren Advincula, 二垒, 天使 选秀顺位：第45顺位（选秀第48顺位）
简评：天使以快速提拔新秀而闻名，而首轮秀Jared Grindlinger——一位双向高中生，也是选秀中最年轻的球员之一——还有一段距离。但Advincula是经验丰富的大学击球手，在加州大学大一之后赢得了开普 Cod 联赛打击王，随后在佐治亚理工大学大三时打击率高达.434。
Mayo：“他是整个选秀中最好的接触型击球手之一。虽然没有太多长打威力，但他从不被三振。他打击时拥有不可思议的选球，有足够的力量让球棒不被震脱手，所以我认为他的能力可以转化。他证明了自己能用木棒击球，所以我认为他会持续发挥，而且他跑垒速度很快。”
Taylor Rabe, 右投, 皇家 选秀顺位：第30顺位（选秀第40顺位）
简评：Rabe是一名运动能力出色的6英尺5英寸右投手，拥有本届最好的速球之一。另一个优势是他的控球，他以7.0的三振保送比在NCAA一级联盟排名第四。这位南卡罗来纳州本地人在2024年接受汤米·约翰手术后恢复，赛季初作为替补先发，后来发展成为密西西比大学的头号先发，并带领球队打入大学世界大赛。
Callis：“皇家非常需要投手。所以当他准备好时，就会得到机会。他在整个大学棒球界拥有最佳的结合了球威和好球率的投手之一。他的速球可达100英里，且控球出色。他的滑球和切球在赛季后期有所进步。所以我认为在堪萨斯城，Taylor Rabe的天赋和机会完美结合了。”
items.author: Jim Callis, Jonathan Mayo and Ben Weinrib

items.titleEn: Cease lands in 2nd in latest Cy Young poll
bodyEn: The races for the AL and NL Cy Young Awards were somewhat volatile during the early months of the season. We started with consensus favorites, then watched other leaders emerge and still other contenders make enough noise to keep things interesting.

But over the past six weeks or so, the races have stabilized to the point where clear favorites are pulling away. This is reflected in the latest Cy Young poll, which shows two familiar names with commanding leads over the rest of the field in their respective leagues.

A total of 33 MLB.com experts cast votes for who they think will take home the hardware after the season based not only on the results from the first half, but also how things are expected to go the rest of the way.

As always, voters were asked to rank their top five picks in each league. Pitchers received vote points on a 5-4-3-2-1 scale -- five points for a first-place vote, four points for a second-place vote and so on. Here are the results.
Unless otherwise noted, stats are through Sunday.
AMERICAN LEAGUE
1. Cam Schlittler, Yankees (154 total vote points; 23 first-place votes)

The AL Cy Young race has been The Cam Schlittler Show for a while. This is the third straight poll in which he's placed first, and it's easy to see why. The All-Star righty (9-6) leads the AL with a 2.20 ERA, a 2.72 FIP and 123 innings pitched, and also leads all AL pitchers with 4.1 bWAR. He's allowed more than three earned runs just twice this season and has held opponents to a .205 average, the best mark in the AL. Meanwhile, his 0.96 WHIP is second-best in the AL by just one percentage point to another pitcher on this list.
2. Dylan Cease, Blue Jays (142 total vote points, 10 first-place votes)

Cease (6-5 after taking a loss on Monday) has been a consistent No. 2 in these polls as he continues to put up impressive numbers. He leads the AL with 155 strikeouts and his .196 opponents' average easily leads the league. The righty missed about two weeks with a hamstring injury in late May/early June, but his six innings Monday give him 104 1/3 on the season, giving him enough to qualify for the ratio leaderboards. As a result, his 2.67 ERA ranks fourth in the AL. In six starts beginning with his return from the IL on June 9, Cease posted a 1.73 ERA to close out the first half.
3. Joe Ryan, Twins (58 total vote points)

Ryan (6-5) is up one spot since the last poll as he continues to be one of the more effective pitchers in the AL. His 2.85 ERA ranks fifth in the league while his 1.05 WHIP ranks sixth. The righty had allowed two earned runs total over his past three outings and allowed 1.4 earned runs per game over his past seven starts, although his most recent outing on Monday night was a rough one in which he allowed eight runs on six homers. And while not overpowering, Ryan has 128 strikeouts in 110 1/3 innings and has averaged 7.6 K's per game since May 20.
4. Sonny Gray, Red Sox (36 total vote points)

If Gray (12-1) were more of a strikeout pitcher, he'd have a shot at the AL's Pitching Triple Crown this season. His 12 wins are tied for most among all MLB pitchers, while his 2.48 ERA ranks second in the AL behind Schlittler. Alas, his 90 strikeouts rank 24th. But that doesn't really matter, because Gray has been dominant in his own way. He's pitched to a 1.11 ERA over his past five starts and has a 1.99 ERA since April 20.
5 (tie). Drew Rasmussen, Rays & Parker Messick, Guardians (26 total vote points apiece)

Rasmussen's 0.95 WHIP is the best in the AL, in large part because he rarely issues walks. The All-Star righty's 1.5 BB/9 ratio is MLB's best, and it helped him enjoy a dominant June in which he had a 0.82 ERA across five starts (33 innings). Overall, Rasmussen (7-5) has a 3.26 ERA, which ranked eighth in the AL entering Monday.

Messick (8-5) has enjoyed a strong rookie season that included a trip to the All-Star Game. The lefty's 2.73 ERA was fourth-best in the AL through Sunday while his .209 opponents' average was tied for second-best. Meanwhile, his 1.07 WHIP ranked seventh. He's allowed more than three earned runs in a start just three times.

Also receiving votes: Tarik Skubal, Tigers; Jacob deGrom, Rangers; Davis Martin, White Sox; Nick Martinez, Rays; Reid Detmers, Angels; Emerson Hancock, Mariners; Gavin Williams, Guardians; Logan Gilbert, Mariners; Ranger Suarez, Red Sox; Bryan Woo, Mariners
NATIONAL LEAGUE
1. Jacob Misiorowski, Brewers (161 total vote points, 30 first-place votes)

Like Schlittler in the AL, The Miz (10-4) appears fixed atop the NL portion of this poll. But that's to be expected after the kind of dominance he's displayed in recent months with his consistent triple-digit heat. Since May 1, a span of 13 starts, Misiorowski has pitched to a 0.87 ERA in 82 1/3 innings and has an MLB-best 13.6 K/9 ratio. He also leads all qualified MLB starters with a 1.57 ERA, a 2.09 FIP, a 0.77 WHIP and 173 strikeouts. With 10 wins so far, he has an outside shot at the MLB Pitching Triple Crown, which hasn't happened in a full season since Johan Santana did it for the Twins in 2006.
2. Cristopher Sánchez, Phillies (113 total vote points, 1 first-place vote)

Sánchez (12-4) has had stretches this season when he looked like the best pitcher in baseball -- most notably when he tossed 50 2/3 scoreless innings, the fifth-longest single-season streak since 1893 and the longest ever by a lefty. Though he came down to Earth a little after that streak ended, he's still one of the toughest pitchers to beat. His 2.71 ERA ranks seventh in the NL, while his 151 strikeouts rank second. In all, he's been worth 5.2 bWAR, the highest mark among all MLB pitchers.
3. Chris Sale, Braves (72 total vote points)

Sale (10-6) seems to get better with age. At 37, he's the oldest pitcher on this list. But he certainly doesn't pitch like it. The 10-time All-Star lefty's 2.06 ERA is second-best in the Majors, and he's not allowed more than three earned runs in a start since early April. The 2024 NL Cy Young winner still maintains a 10.5 K/9 ratio and his 123 strikeouts rank seventh in the NL.
4. Zack Wheeler, Phillies (41 total vote points, 1 first-place vote)

Wheeler's 2.13 ERA would rank third in the Majors if he had the innings to qualify. The righty (10-1) didn't make his season debut until late April after having thoracic outlet surgery last September, but he's pitched like the prime version of himself this season, even if he doesn't yet show up on leaderboards. His 0.89 WHIP would rank second in the league, as would his .185 opponents' average.
5. Chase Burns, Reds (39 total vote points)

Burns (11-1) has consistently shown why the Reds wanted to sign him to a seven-year, $105 million contract extension. From April 16 through June 21, a stretch of 12 starts, the hard-throwing righty pitched to a 1.69 ERA with 84 strikeouts in 69 1/3 innings. His 11 wins are tied for second-most in the NL while his 2.54 ERA ranks third. Meanwhile, opponents are hitting .206 against him, which ranks fourth in the NL.

Also receiving votes: Yoshinobu Yamamoto, Dodgers; Paul Skenes, Pirates (1 first-place vote); Jesus Luzardo, Phillies; Max Meyer, Marlins; Mason Miller, Padres; Eduardo Rodriguez, D-backs; Foster Griffin, Nationals; Braxton Ashcraft, Pirates
items.titleZh: Cease在最新赛扬奖票选中位列第二
items.summaryZh: 最新赛扬奖票选中，蓝鸟投手Dylan Cease排名美联第二。
bodyZh: 本赛季初期，美联和国联赛扬奖的竞争有些动荡。我们一开始有共识的热门人选，然后看到其他领先者出现，还有更多的竞争者制造了足够的声音，让事情变得有趣。
但在过去六周左右，竞争已经稳定下来，明确的领先者开始脱颖而出。这反映在最新的赛扬奖票选中，两位熟悉的名字在各自联盟中遥遥领先。
共有33位MLB.com专家参与了投票，他们根据上半年的表现以及对下半年的预期，选出了他们认为赛季结束后将赢得该奖项的投手。
一如既往，投票者被要求在每个联盟中选出前五名。投手按5-4-3-2-1的评分体系获得票数——第一名获得5分，第二名4分，以此类推。以下是结果。
除非另有说明，统计数据截止到周日。
美国联盟
1. Cam Schlittler, 洋基 (总分154分；23张第一名选票)
美联赛扬奖竞争长期以来都是Cam Schlittler的表演。这是他连续第三次位居榜首，原因显而易见。这位全明星右投手（9胜6负）以2.20的自责分率、2.72的FIP和123局的投球领跑美联，同时以4.1的bWAR领先所有美联投手。他本赛季仅两次失分超过3分，并将对手打击率压制在.205，为美联最佳。此外，他的0.96的WHIP在美联排名第二，仅落后于本榜单上的另一位投手。
2. Dylan Cease, 蓝鸟 (总分142分，10张第一名选票)

Cease（周一输球后战绩为6胜5负）在这些票选中一直稳居第二，他继续交出令人印象深刻的数据。他以155次三振领跑美联，.196的对手打击率也轻松领跑联盟。这位右投手在5月底/6月初因腿筋伤势错过了大约两周，但周一他投了6局，使赛季总投球局数达到104又1/3局，足以进入比率排行榜。因此，他的2.67的自责分率在美联排名第四。从6月9日伤愈复出后的6场先发中，Cease在赛季前半段结束时交出了1.73的自责分率。
3. Joe Ryan, 双城 (总分58分)
Ryan（6胜5负）比上次票选上升了一个名次，他仍然是美联最有效的投手之一。他的2.85的自责分率在联盟排名第五，1.05的WHIP排名第六。这位右投手在过去三场比赛中总共只失了2分自责分，在过去七场先发中平均每场失1.4分自责分，尽管最近一次先发（周一晚上）表现糟糕，被击出6支本垒打失8分。虽然并非统治级，Ryan在110又1/3局中投出128次三振，自5月20日以来场均7.6次三振。
4. Sonny Gray, 红袜 (总分36分)
如果Gray（12胜1负）更像三振型投手，他本有机会争夺美联投手三冠王。他的12胜与所有MLB投手并列最多，2.48的自责分率在美联仅次于Schlittler。然而，他的90次三振仅排名第24。但这并不重要，因为Gray以自己的方式保持统治力。他在过去五场先发中自责分率仅为1.11，自4月20日以来自责分率为1.99。
5（并列）. Drew Rasmussen, 光芒 & Parker Messick, 守护者 (各26分)
Rasmussen的0.95的WHIP是美联最佳，很大程度上是因为他很少保送。这位全明星右投手的1.5的BB/9比率是MLB最佳，这帮助他在六月份取得统治级表现，五场先发（33局）自责分率为0.82。总体而言，Rasmussen（7胜5负）的自责分率为3.26，截至周一在美联排名第八。
Messick（8胜5负）度过了一个强势的新秀赛季，包括入选全明星赛。这位左投手截至周日的2.73的自责分率在美联排名第四，.209的对手打击率并列第二。同时，他的1.07的WHIP排名第七。他仅在三场先发中失分超过3分。
其他获得选票的投手：Tarik Skubal, 老虎；Jacob deGrom, 游骑兵；Davis Martin, 白袜；Nick Martinez, 光芒；Reid Detmers, 天使；Emerson Hancock, 水手；Gavin Williams, 守护者；Logan Gilbert, 水手；Ranger Suarez, 红袜；Bryan Woo, 水手
国家联盟
1. Jacob Misiorowski, 酿酒人 (总分161分，30张第一名选票)

就像美联的Schlittler一样，The Miz（10胜4负）在国联的票选中似乎稳坐榜首。这并不意外，因为他最近几个月展现了持续三位数火球的统治力。自5月1日以来的13场先发中，Misiorowski在82又1/3局内投出0.87的自责分率，13.6的K/9比率是MLB最佳。他还以1.57的自责分率、2.09的FIP、0.77的WHIP和173次三振领先所有合格MLB先发投手。目前已有10胜，他有机会冲击MLB投手三冠王，自2006年Johan Santana为双城队做到以来，还没有人在完整赛季中实现过。
2. Cristopher Sánchez, 费城人 (总分113分，1张第一名选票)
Sánchez（12胜4负）本赛季有些时段看起来像是棒球界最佳投手——最引人注目的是他连续50又2/3局无失分，这是自1893年以来单赛季第五长的连续无失分纪录，也是左投手最长纪录。虽然那段纪录结束后他有所回落，但他仍然是最难击败的投手之一。他的2.71的自责分率在国联排名第七，151次三振排名第二。总体而言，他的bWAR为5.2，是所有MLB投手中最高的。
3. Chris Sale, 勇士 (总分72分)
Sale（10胜6负）似乎越老越强。37岁的他是本榜单上最年长的投手，但他的投球却不像这个年龄。这位10届全明星左投手的2.06的自责分率在大联盟排名第二，自四月初以来没有在一场先发中失分超过3分。2024年国联赛扬奖得主仍然保持着10.5的K/9比率，123次三振在国联排名第七。
4. Zack Wheeler, 费城人 (总分41分，1张第一名选票)
Wheeler的2.13的自责分率如果局数足够进入排行榜，将在大联盟排名第三。这位右投手（10胜1负）直到四月底才完成赛季首秀（去年九月接受了 thoracic outlet 手术），但他本赛季投出了巅峰版本的表现，尽管尚未出现在排行榜上。他的0.89的WHIP将在联盟排名第二，.185的对手打击率也将排名第二。
5. Chase Burns, 红人 (总分39分)
Burns（11胜1负）持续证明为什么红人队想与他签下七年1.05亿美元的续约合同。从4月16日到6月21日的12场先发中，这位火球右投手在69又1/3局内投出1.69的自责分率，84次三振。他的11胜在国联并列第二，2.54的自责分率排名第三。同时，对手对他的打击率为.206，在国联排名第四。
其他获得选票的投手：Yoshinobu Yamamoto, 道奇；Paul Skenes, 海盗（1张第一名选票）；Jesus Luzardo, 费城人；Max Meyer, 马林鱼；Mason Miller, 教士；Eduardo Rodriguez, 响尾蛇；Foster Griffin, 国民；Braxton Ashcraft, 海盗
items.author: Jason Foster

items.titleEn: Frustrations build as Blue Jays' offense continues to struggle, Cease falters
bodyEn: TORONTO -- Day by day, you can feel the pressure building around and amongst the Blue Jays.

The 100th game of the season brought another loss, this time a 7-1 defeat by the Rays, and the Blue Jays don’t exactly have 62 games to work with here. The Trade Deadline looms just 13 games away on Aug. 3, and the Blue Jays are tilting themselves in the wrong direction at 46-54.
This is what mattered most from Monday’s loss:
1. Frustrations are building …

Soon, there may come a day when bats are broken and dugouts are redecorated in fits of rage. For now, we’re left with the early signs of frustration from the Blue Jays, a team staring down their own mortality.

Vladimir Guerrero Jr.’s at-bat in the third inning captured this so well. Guerrero turned on the first pitch he saw and launched it to left field, for what finally looked like his first home run at Rogers Centre this season. Guerrero didn’t get all of it, though, and when it fell short at the base of the wall into Chandler Simpson’s glove, there was a feeling of … “of course.”
